situation.title: When
situation.prompt: What's the customer's circumstance? What just happened to trigger this need?
examples: Friday 4pm, my CFO just asked for next quarter's variance report by Monday
On a train with patchy 4G, a contract needs approving before the next stop
Monday morning, opening the dashboard and realising the export is empty again

motivation.title: I want to
motivation.prompt: What does the customer want to do? Lead with a verb. (Functional, emotional, or social.)
examples: Cut my reporting prep from 6 hours to under 1 (functional)
Look prepared in front of my CFO without overexplaining (social)

ideal-outcome.title: So I can
ideal-outcome.prompt: What progress is the customer trying to make? Write outcomes as action verbs.
examples: Decide which two of five vendors to call back
Walk into the exec review with a one-page summary already done
Be seen as the PM whose product reviews are always on schedule

emotional-social.title: Emotional + Social jobs
emotional-social.prompt: For each functional Job Story above, name at least one emotional and one social companion job; these can later map to Bain value elements.
examples: Emotional — Feel calm during close week instead of dreading it
Emotional — Be reassured the numbers are right without re-checking
Social — Be seen by my CFO as someone who actually owns the numbers
